title: Reigning MVP Shai Gilgeous-Alexander and the Thunder begin their quest for a repeat against the Suns
description: The Oklahoma City Thunder, no longer the young upstarts, begin defense of their NBA title Sunday at home against the Phoenix Suns. The Thunder remain one of the youngest teams in the league. But now, reigning MVP Shai Gilgeous-Alexander leads a group of grizzled postseason veterans that won two seven-game playoff series last year, including the NBA Finals against the Indiana Pacers. Phoenix finished 45-37 in the regular season and reached the quarterfinals of the NBA Cup. The Suns beat Golden State on Friday night to earn the No. 8 seed. Oklahoma City went 3-2 against Phoenix this season.

title: A stacked field and a tailwind could mean another year of fast times at the Boston Marathon
description: Defending champion Sharon Lokedi is back at the Boston Marathon, and a course record could be in play. Cool weather and a tailwind could lead to fast times again after Lokedi beat the previous mark by more than 2 1/2 minutes in 2025. Reigning men’s champion and fellow Kenyan John Korir is also back to lead a field of more than 30,000 on the 26.2-mile race to Boston’s Copley Square. The race says it has its strongest field ever, with seven of the top men's finishers from last year and eight women with personal bests that would have beaten the Boston course record until a year ago.

title: Metallurgical Engineering Student Earns $25,000 ELLWOOD Scholarship
description: RAPID CITY, SD – What began as a backyard experiment with rebar and a broken cinder block has come full circle for South Dakota Mines student Caleb Oppelt.  Oppelt, a […]

title: Mitchell scores 32 as Cavaliers defeat Raptors 126-113 in Game 1 of East first-round series
description: Donovan Mitchell scored 32 points, Max Strus had 24 off the bench and the Cleveland Cavaliers defeated the Toronto Raptors 126-113 in Game 1 of their Eastern Conference first-round playoff series. James Harden had 22 points and 10 assists while Evan Mobley had 17 and seven rebounds for fourth-seeded Cleveland, which hosts Game 2 on Monday night. Mitchell has scored at least 30 points in an NBA-record nine straight series openers. RJ Barrett scored 24 points and Scottie Barnes had 21 for the Raptors, who were playing in their first playoff game since 2022. Toronto was missing point guard Immanuel Quickley because of a mild right hamstring strain.

title: Royals manager Matt Quatraro ejected after top of first inning in game against the Yankees
description: Kansas City manager Matt Quatraro was ejected before Royals starter Noah Cameron threw a pitch in the first inning against the New York Yankees. After Maikel Garcia was stranded at second base and nearly picked off by New York’s Will Warren, Quatraro raced out to start arguing with umpires Nestor Ceja and Chris Guccione. The argument did not appear heated initially, but when Quatraro turned away he was tossed. Bench coach Paul Hoover took over for Quatraro.

title: Rapid City Vinyl Fans Line-Up for Record Store Day
description: RAPID CITY, SD – A line of music enthusiasts stretched around the block in downtown Rapid City on Saturday as collectors gathered for the annual celebration of Record Store Day. […]

title: A big midterms year in Arizona kicks off with the state’s largest county embroiled in election drama
description: Much of the political spotlight this election year in Arizona has been focused on the office that runs elections in Maricopa County, the state’s largest. Republican Justin Heap is overseeing elections in the Phoenix-area and he's an election skeptic. Heap is in a legal feud with the county board over election procedures. He's put in place a controversial system for checking signatures on mail ballots. And he's run voter files through a federal system to check for noncitizens despite questions about the system's accuracy. His actions have drawn pushback from members of the county board, as well as rebukes from the attorney general and secretary of state.

title: The Lyrid meteor shower is visible now and peaking soon. Here’s how to spot it
description: The Lyrid meteor shower is expected to bring fiery streaks to the night sky. The shower peaks Tuesday night into Wednesday morning. Ten to twenty meteors are generally visible per hour. The show will be visible across the globe, but views will be best in the Northern Hemisphere. Meteor showers happen when Earth plows through debris trails left behind by space rocks. To see the Lyrids, go outside after midnight and look to the northeastern sky. Avoid looking at your phone to let your eyes get used to the night sky. The meteors will look like trails of light.

title: Taste the Room: Pour 54 Taphouse
description: Type: Taphouse Price: $$ Location: 615 Main St, Rapid City, SD Pour 54 Taphouse: The Bar Experience Finally Caught Up With Us Think back to the last time you stood […]

title: Welcome to Kentucky. Please Enjoy the Whiskey…and the Fungus
description: You found it. The house you’ve been picturing for years. Quiet roads, rolling hills, and a short walk from one of Kentucky’s legendary distilleries. On a still evening, the air […]

title: Trump’s dyslexia barbs stir anguish and anger, perpetuating a myth about the learning disability
description: Some of the harshest words that President Donald Trump has used against a leading Democratic rival aren't about political differences but rather a learning disability. Trump's insults geared toward California Gov. Gavin Newsom's struggle with dyslexia are stirring anguish and anger from the president's supporters and critics. Advocates for people with dyslexia are concerned Trump's comments perpetuate a myth about dyslexia. Trump has called the governor “stupid,” “low IQ,” “mentally disabled” and unfit to become president. Dyslexia affects the link between the brain and printed language, and a large body of research has found that dyslexia and IQ are unrelated.

title: Indiana primary will test Trump’s control over Republican Party after redistricting defiance
description: President Donald Trump's influence on the Republican Party is facing a test in Indiana's upcoming primary. After state senators opposed Trump's push for redistricting, he endorsed seven primary challengers in the May 5 election. One Republican incumbent being challenged is Spencer Deery. He resisted Trump's pressure, and now he's facing nearly $1 million in campaign spending against him. Deery says he works for his constituents, not Trump. Indiana's governor, Republican Mike Braun, says he supports the challengers not because of redistricting but because he needs their help to advance his agenda. Meanwhile, one former governor, Republican Mitch Daniels, is working to back incumbents like Deery who've drawn Trump's ire.

title: Boyd, Smith Capture School Records in Day Two in Southern California
description: The Black Hills State University men’s and women’s track & field teams continued action in day two of their trip to southern California, which included two school records, four top-10 […]

title: Angels ace José Soriano has an 0.28 ERA, 39 Ks after 5 phenomenal starts to begin the season
description: In just five starts, José Soriano’s season with the Los Angeles Angels has gone from good to great to historic. Soriano pitched two-hit ball into the sixth inning of the Angels’ 8-0 victory over the Padres, ending San Diego’s eight-game winning streak with yet another dominant outing by Los Angeles’ right-handed Dominican ace. Soriano has an ERA of 0.28 after allowing just one run in his first 32 2/3 innings this season. He leads the majors with 39 strikeouts while allowing only 11 hits, and he’s tied with Milwaukee’s Aaron Ashby for the lead with five wins.

title: Green scores 36 points, Suns lock down Warriors, Curry in 111-96 win in NBA’s play-in tourney
description: Jalen Green scored 36 points, Devin Booker added 20 and the Phoenix Suns locked down Stephen Curry and the Golden State Warriors, winning 111-96 in the NBA’s play-in tournament Friday night. The Suns took the No. 8 seed in the Western Conference playoffs and will face the defending NBA champion Oklahoma City Thunder on Sunday in Game 1. Green shot 14 of 20 from the field, including 8 of 14 on 3-pointers. Booker and Golden State’s Draymond Green were both assessed two technical fouls late in the fourth quarter after exchanging words multiple times and were ejected. The 38-year-old Curry couldn’t get many clean looks and finished with 17 points on 4-of-16 shooting. Brandin Podziemski led the Warriors with 23 points.

title: Stories of Black and Indigenous patriots come into focus as US remembers the American Revolution
description: The stories of Black and Indigenous men who fought during the American Revolution are sometimes overlooked. History books traditionally focus on the roles of white leaders such as George Washington and Paul Revere. As America prepares to celebrate its 250th anniversary, patriots of color are being celebrated through programs nationwide that aim to tell a more complete story of the birth of the nation. Christopher Brown, a historian at Columbia University, says “a more accurate view of the past” has emerged that showcases the diverse collection of men and women who played critical roles in the fight for freedom. The National Park Service estimates 5,500 patriots of color served on the colonial side.

title: Magic rout Hornets 121-90 in play-in game, advance to face Pistons in 1st round of playoffs
description: Paolo Banchero scored 25 points and the Orlando Magic built a 35-point lead in the first half on the way to a 121-90 rout of Charlotte Hornets in a play-in tournament game on Friday night and advanced to a first-round playoff matchup against the Detroit Pistons. The Magic earned the No. 8 seed in playoffs and will start their best-of-seven series at Detroit on Sunday. LaMelo Ball scored 21 of his 23 points in the third quarter for Charlotte. Banchero had 12 points and Wendell Carter Jr. added 10 as the Hornets shot 5 for 20 with six turnovers in the first quarter and fell behind 38-16.

title: Rapper Tory Lanez sues California prison system for $100 million over stabbing by inmate
description: Rapper Tory Lanez has sued the California prison system, claiming he should not have been housed with an inmate who stabbed him 16 times last year. Lanez's legal name is Daystar Peterson. He filed the federal lawsuit Tuesday, seeking $100 million in damages. He alleges that the attack was unprovoked and life-threatening. Lanez is serving 10 years for shooting hip-hop star Megan Thee Stallion in the feet. The lawsuit says prison officials were slow to respond and failed to use measures to stop the attack. It also claims his notebooks with unpublished lyrics were unlawfully seized. A corrections spokesperson says the agency does not comment on pending litigation.

title: US and Cuban officials met recently in Havana amid new diplomatic push
description: An American delegation has met recently with Cuban government officials in the island nation. It marks a renewed diplomatic push even as U.S. President Donald Trump has threatened to intervene in Cuba and its leader said this week that his country is prepared to fight if that should happen. A senior State Department official met with the grandson of retired Cuban leader Raúl Castro last week during the trip. That's according to a department official who spoke Friday on condition of anonymity to discuss the sensitive matter. The official didn't say who from the U.S. met with Raúl Guillermo Rodríguez Castro. A second U.S. official said Secretary of State Marco Rubio was not part of the delegation that visited Havana.

title: In The Running: Kevin Maher, Ward 3
description: The race for City Council is on. Here is questionnaire sent to those running to help you understand the candidates. Who are you, in your daily life? I am a […]

title: A lead prosecutor in John Brennan investigation has been removed from case, AP source says
description: A lead prosecutor in the John Brennan investigation has been removed from the case after expressing concerns to Justice Department officials about the viability of a potential criminal prosecution of the former CIA director. That's according to a person familiar with the matter. Maria Medetis Long told defense lawyers involved in the investigation that she was no longer participating in the Brennan investigation. Her departure from the investigation came after she conveyed doubt that there was sufficient evidence for a criminal case against Brennan. The Justice Department did not dispute that Medetis Long was no longer part of the investigation but also did not elaborate on the circumstances.

title: Roommates of man accused of killing 2 say a dispute preceded the Atlanta-area attacks
description: Authorities have charged Olaolukitan Adon Abel with killing two people and injuring another in the Atlanta area. Roommates told The Associated Press that he stormed out shortly before the attacks early Monday morning after an argument over air conditioning. The victims include a Department of Homeland Security auditor and a homeless 49-year-old man. Adon Abel is a U.K. native and a U.S. Navy veteran who was granted U.S. citizenship in 2022. He faces numerous charges, including two counts of malice murder. Federal prosecutors on Friday said that a homeless 35-year-old man bought the gun for Abel in February. Homeland Security Secretary Markwayne Mullin has raised concerns about Abel's citizenship, given his criminal history.

title: Federal Courts
description: Court cases can take place in federal court, state court, or tribal court.  This week’s column will discuss the role of federal courts. The District of South Dakota (D.S.D.) is […]

title: Matt Fitzpatrick rides a good bounce and great golf to a 63 to lead Viktor Hovland at Hilton Head
description: Matt Fitzpatrick has a one-shot lead over Viktor Hovland going into the weekend at the RBC Heritage. Fitzpatrick took advantage of one big break when his shot on the par-3 14th caromed onto the green and narrowly avoided the water. He made a 30-footer for birdie. But it was mostly great golf that led to 63. Hovland played in the tough afternoon wind for a 65. Harris English was four behind. Fitzpatrick played bogey-free on a day of 20 double bogeys for the rest of the field. Scottie Scheffler hit all 14 fairways and shot 67. He was seven back.

title: Dallas Stars and Minnesota Wild finally set for Game 1 in long-expected 1st-round NHL playoff series
description: The Dallas Stars and Minnesota Wild are getting ready to start a first-round playoff series that has been expected for months. Game 1 is Saturday in Dallas between the teams that spent most of the season second and third in the Central Division behind President’s Trophy winner Colorado. The Stars and Wild each won twice and scored 13 goals over their four games during the regular season. Dallas pretty much secured home-ice with a 5-4 win over the Wild on April 9, a game with 12 roughing penalties. Both teams boast 40-goal scorers, with Kirill Kaprizov and Matt Boldy for the Wild, and Jason Robertson and Wyatt Johnston for the Stars.

title: Kevin Durant, LeBron James meet in playoffs for 4th time when Rockets visit short-handed Lakers
description: LeBron James and Kevin Durant have faced each other in the playoffs three times, and the superstar with the superior supporting cast won a championship every time. That's why Durant and his Houston Rockets are favored over James' Los Angeles Lakers when they meet for the fourth time this month. Luka Doncic and Austin Reaves are sidelined indefinitely with injuries, leaving James to lead another team against a superior opponent. Durant is hoping his first postseason with the Rockets will be memorable. Game 1 is Saturday night in Los Angeles.

title: Affordability Dominates Dakota Town Hall Debate
description: From the latest episode of the Dakota Town Hall podcast, South Dakota political leaders and candidates zeroed in on affordability, voter turnout and the state’s ongoing property tax debate as […]

title: Ballesteros, Hoerner and Happ homer as Cubs hand Mets their 9th straight loss in 12-4 romp
description: Moisés Ballesteros, Nico Hoerner and Ian Happ homered, and the Chicago Cubs handed the Mets their ninth straight loss, pounding New York 12-4. The Mets’ skid is their worst since the 2004 team dropped 11 straight. They’ve been outscored 56-16 during the streak. Chicago scored in double digits for the third straight game and major league-leading fourth time this season. Ballesteros made it 4-0 in the first with a three-run homer. Hoerner had three hits and drove in two runs after setting a career high with five RBIs in Wednesday’s 11-2 win at Philadelphia. The Mets' Kodai Senga was tagged for seven runs in his second straight start.

title: Traffic and trepidation in the Persian Gulf could keep gasoline prices from dropping quickly
description: Oil prices plunged and the stock market rallied after U.S. President Donald Trump and Iran’s foreign minister said that the Strait of Hormuz is fully open. Drivers are hoping that freeing oil tankers to leave the Persian Gulf will lead to lower gasoline prices. But energy experts say it could take months for prices to fall to levels enjoyed before the war began. The speed oil tankers travel from ports to refineries, traffic in the strait, lingering security concerns and damage to energy infrastructure in the Middle East are all playing a role in the price of gasoline.

title: Rapid City mayor pushes parole reform
description: RAPID CITY, S.D. — Concerns about repeat offenders on parole took center stage in the latest episode of the “Meeting with the Mayor” podcast, where Mayor Jason Salamun discussed growing […]

title: Rapid City Gun Show
description: Rapid City, S.D. Firearm enthusiasts, collectors and vendors from across the region are expected to gather in Rapid City this weekend for the Dakota Territory Gun Collectors Association’s annual Sonny […]

title: Now with Atlanta, Angel Reese says she is grateful for her 2 years in Chicago but wanted more
description: Angel Reese is excited about the talent she'll be surrounded with in Atlanta. The star forward had her introductory news conference with the Dream on Friday. Reese was acquired in a trade with the Chicago Sky. She says she was grateful for her time in Chicago but she wanted more. She says she wants to win and to be surrounded by people who can help her improve. Reese led the WNBA in rebounds in each of her two seasons in Chicago. She joins a team that won a franchise-record 30 games last season under coach Karl Smesko.

title: The 36th annual Deadwood Jam is bringing a major country music act to the Black Hills this fall.
description: Deadwood, SD – Organizers announced that Ian Munsick will headline the opening night of the free music festival on Friday, Sept. 18, at the Sue Lundberg Memorial Stage in Outlaw […]

title: Former Wisconsin man sentenced to 20 months in federal prison for illegal campaign contributions
description: A federal judge has sentenced a former Wisconsin man to 20 months in prison for making campaign contributions after renouncing his U.S. citizenship. According to prosecutors, Roger Hoffman of Madison renounced his U.S. citizenship in 2009 after becoming a citizen of the Caribbean nation Saint Kitts and Nevis. He continued making political contributions using a straw donor to get around laws prohibiting foreign nationals from influencing U.S. elections. According to a November 2021 indictment, he unlawfully funneled more than $400,000 into state and federal elections over more than a decade. Court documents do not list specific recipients. Hoffman's attorney didn't return a message.

title: Democrats fail to field candidates for a majority of South Dakota legislative seats
description: Campaign signs in Sioux Falls, South Dakota, in November 2024. (Photo by Makenzie Huber/South Dakota Searchlight) Democrats are running for 46 of South Dakota’s 105 legislative seats — leaving 56% […]

title: Judge declines to dismiss case in 1979 disappearance of Etan Patz, setting up 3rd trial
description: The murder case surrounding the 1979 disappearance of 6-year-old Etan Patz is on track for a third trial. A New York judge declined Friday to dismiss charges against a onetime clerk charged with abducting and killing the boy on his way to school. The man, Pedro Hernandez, has been behind bars since his 2012 arrest. Etan’s disappearance became one of the United States’ most well known missing child cases. He was among the first vanished kids to be pictured on milk cartons, and the May 25 anniversary of his disappearance became National Missing Children’s Day.

title: Texans’ Will Anderson Jr. lands a record $150M extension, top-paid non-QB in NFL
description: Houston Texans defensive end Will Anderson Jr. has agreed to a three-year, $150 million contract extension making him the highest paid non-quarterback in NFL history. That's according to two people with knowledge of the deal told who spoke to The Associated Press on condition of anonymity Friday because the deal had not yet been announced. The extension includes $134 million in guaranteed money. The $50 million per year Anderson will earn surpasses the $46.5 million per year defensive end Micah Parsons got when he signed a four-year extension with the Packers after a trade from Dallas last year.

title: American Cancer Society patient transportation and lodging grants awarded to Monument Health
description: RAPID CITY, S.D. (April 17, 2026) – The American Cancer Society (ACS) has awarded up to $30,000 in grants to Monument Health Cancer Care Institute to help ease the burden for patients who experience challenges with the expense of transportation and lodging to get to their cancer related medical appointments. This is the fifth year Cancer […]

title: Tentative contract deal holds off strike by New York City apartment building workers
description: Union leaders representing thousands of New York City apartment house doorpersons, superintendents and other workers have reached a tentative contract agreement with building owners. The union, called 32BJ SEIU, announced the deal Friday. It averts a strike at the homes of an estimated 1.5 million people. The nearly 34,000 workers’ contract with an array of private building owners would have expired at midnight Monday. A strike would have been the first in 35 years. The tentative contract will head to union members for a vote.

title: Federal judge dismisses DOJ lawsuit seeking detailed information about Rhode Island voters
description: A federal judge in Rhode Island has dismissed the Trump administration's lawsuit demanding the state's unredacted voter rolls. U.S. District Court Judge Mary McElroy on Friday sided with Rhode Island election officials and civil rights advocates, stating federal law doesn't allow such a "fishing expedition." The Justice Department declined to comment. The DOJ claims it needs the data for election security, but officials argue the demand violates privacy laws and are concerned it will be used to check citizenship status. The Rhode Island ruling is similar to other rejections by federal judges across the country since the Justice Department began seeking detailed voter data from the states.

title: Trump’s lawyers are in talks with the IRS to resolve president’s $10B lawsuit
description: The Trump administration says it’s in talks with the IRS to resolve a $10 billion lawsuit over leaked tax information. Tax and ethics experts say the lawsuit raises legal and ethical questions. In a Friday federal court filing, President Donald Trump requests an extension to potentially resolve or narrow the dispute. Trump filed the lawsuit in a Florida federal court earlier this year, alleging reputational and financial harm from the leak. Former IRS contractor Charles Edward Littlejohn was sentenced to five years in prison in 2024 for leaking tax information about Trump and others.

title: Rapid Acceleration Team 4593 Qualifies for FIRST World Championship, Seeks Community Support to Reach Houston
description: A Rapid City robotics team is preparing to represent South Dakota on the world stage—but they need the community’s help to get there. 🚀 Rapid Acceleration Team 4593, the Black […]

title: Judge sides with Arizona election official in ruling that has implications for midterms voting
description: The top election official in Arizona’s most populous county will get more authority in running elections after a judge sided with his office in a prolonged legal fight with the county board that shares responsibility for overseeing voting. Justin Heap, the Republican recorder in Maricopa County, sued the predominantly Republican county board of supervisors last summer, alleging it had illegally taken control of certain aspects of election administration. Superior Court Judge Scott Blaney largely sided with Heap’s office in his ruling, which was filed Thursday but appeared on the public docket Friday. The decision could have broad implications in one of the nation’s biggest battleground states, which will have several high-profile races this fall.

title: White House chief of staff to meet with Anthropic CEO over its new AI technology
description: White House Chief of Staff Susie Wiles plans to meet with Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei to discuss the company's new AI model, Mythos. The model has gained attention for its advanced capabilities and potential use by the federal government. A White House official says the administration is engaging with AI labs about its models. The planned meeting Friday follows tensions between the Trump administration and Anthropic over the use of AI technology in federal agencies. Anthropic has limited the Mythos model's use to select customers due to its ability to surpass human cybersecurity experts in finding vulnerabilities.

title: New oil and gas lease sale set for Alaska’s Arctic National Wildlife Refuge, amid litigation
description: The U.S. government plans another oil and gas lease sale for Alaska’s Arctic National Wildlife Refuge. The sale set for June 5 follows two prior sales that saw no interest from major oil companies. It also comes amid ongoing litigation aimed at blocking drilling in a region seen as sacred by the indigenous Gwich’in. A law passed by Congress last year calls for four lease sales in the refuge's coastal plain over a 10-year period. This would be the first sale for the refuge under that law.

title: The Field Never Sleeps: Agricultural Markets Pivot to 24/7 Trading
description: Those with dirt under their fingernails—and the brokers who represent them—are sounding the alarm CHICAGO. IL – The rhythmic opening bell of the Chicago Board of Trade, a tradition spanning […]

title: Prosecutors say makeup, wigs helped former Alabama tackle impersonate NFL players in $20M fraud
description: A former defensive tackle at the University of Alabama is accused of wearing makeup and wigs to impersonate NFL players in a fraud scheme that bilked investors of $20 million. Federal prosecutors in Atlanta charged Luther Davis last month with conspiracy to commit wire fraud and aggravated identity theft. An email and call to his attorney, Gabe Banks, on Friday were not immediately returned. According to a court filing, Davis is expected to plead guilty later this month. In a criminal complaint, prosecutors say Davis impersonated three separate NFL players. The complaint only identifies the NFL players by their initials.

title: Investigation Underway After Mountain Goat Mutilated in Spearfish Canyon
description: SPEARFISH, S.D. – South Dakota wildlife officials are investigating a recent case of illegal scavenging in Spearfish Canyon. On April 8, a deceased mountain goat was discovered at the base […]

title: Transgender athlete focuses on what may be her last track season as Supreme Court ruling looms
description: A West Virginia transgender girl who competes on her high school girls' track team says she's focused on having fun with her friends. Becky Pepper-Jackson isn't dwelling on her case that was heard before the U.S. Supreme Court. In January, the court’s conservative majority signaled it would rule that state bans on transgender athletes in girls' and women's sports don’t violate either the Constitution or the federal law known as Title IX. Such a ruling would bring an end to Pepper-Jackson's high school sports career. She finished third in the state meet a year ago in the discus as a freshman. The justices are expected to rule by early summer.

title: Garret Anderson, the talented outfielder and Angels career hits leader, dies at 53
description: Former Angels outfielder Garret Anderson has died. He was 53. The multitalented player became the Angels' career hits leader and led the team to its only World Series title. The Angels announced Anderson’s death without immediately disclosing the cause or location. Anderson reached the majors with the then-California Angels in 1994 and played for the club until 2008, primarily as a left fielder. He was a fixture in the heart of their batting order for his entire tenure, becoming the franchise's leader in hits, RBIs and games played among many other achievements. Anderson was a three-time AL All-Star from 2002-05.

title: Elsie Marie Woodard
description: Elsie Marie Palander was born June 10, 1937 to Carmelita and Carl Palander, the third of four children on a homestead 12 miles north of Murdo, South Dakota in the […]

title: Betty May Ryan
description: Betty May Ryan, 89, Lead, SD passed away Tuesday, April 14, 2026 at Edgewood Healthcare, Spearfish. Graveside Services will be held at 11:00 AM, Friday, May 8, at Mountain Lawn […]

title: Kevin Durant returns to the playoffs, leading the Rockets into a 1st-round clash with the Lakers
description: Kevin Durant is back in the playoffs after missing out last season. In his first season in Houston after this summer’s blockbuster trade from Phoenix, Durant leads the young Rockets in their first-round series with the Los Angeles Lakers beginning Saturday night. The 37-year-old Durant missed the last seven games of the regular season last year with an ankle injury as the Suns went 1-6 to fall out of playoff contention. This season he finished second in the league by playing 2,840 minutes despite being in his 19th NBA season.

title: Spearfish boys basketball coach going to Sioux Falls
description: SPEARFISH, S.D. – Spearfish boys basketball coach Ben Schultz has announced he is leaving the Spartan program and taking over head coaching duties at Sioux Falls Roosevelt. Schultz made the […]

description: RAPID CITY, S.D. – Eighteen regional finalists will gather in Rapid City to test their knowledge of American government during the local National Civics Bee competition on Saturday, April 18. […]

description: UNDATED – South Dakota high school baseball scores for Thursday, April 16: Pierre 15 Smittys 11 F Tri-Valley 18 Garretson 3 F Dell Rapids 15 Baltic 0 F Watertown 10 […]

title: Records broken at annual Track-O-Rama in Rapid City
description: RAPID CITY, S.D. – Athletes from around the area gathered in Rapid City Thursday for the annual Track-O-Rama held at O’Harra Stadium. Several new meet records were set, along with […]

title: The Supreme Court hands a win to oil and gas companies fighting environmental lawsuits in Louisiana
description: The Supreme Court is handing a win to oil and gas companies fighting lawsuits over coastal land loss and environmental degradation in Louisiana. The procedural decision gives the companies a new day in federal court after a state jury ordered Chevron to pay upward of $740 million to clean up damage to the state’s coastline, one of multiple similar lawsuits. The companies were backed by the Trump administration and argued that the case belongs in federal court because they began oil production and refining during World War II as U.S. contractors. Louisiana’s coastal parishes have lost more than 2,000 square miles of land over the past century.

title: Police Partner with Ring To Solve Crimes
description: Pennington County, S.D. – The Rapid City Police Department and the Pennington County Sheriff’s Office have partnered with Ring, a popular manufacturer of home security video systems, in order to […]

title: Meade 46-1 holds question-and-answer session on Homeschool Connection Program
description: STURGIS, S.D. – The Meade 46-1 School District held an informational meeting Wednesday night to answer questions about the Homeschool Connection Program. The program offers curriculum and academic support for […]

title: Don Schlitz, storied country songwriter behind such hits as ‘The Gambler,’ dies at 73
description: Don Schlitz, the storied country music songwriter known for “The Gambler,” died Thursday at a Nashville hospital. He was 73. The cause of death was not immediately known. A press release from the Grand Ole Opry describes it as a sudden illness. The two-time Grammy Award winner was inducted into the Songwriters Hall of Fame, the Nashville Songwriters Hall of Fame and the Country Music Hall of Fame. He made his Grand Ole Opry debut in 2017 and was later inducted in 2022. The historic venue’s Saturday night show will be dedicated in Schlitz’s honor. Schlitz wrote songs recorded by Kenny Rogers, Randy Travis and The Judds.

title: Husted Wins Goat Tying at Jackrabbit Stampede
description: The Black Hills State University men’s and women’s rodeo teams were back in action for the second time this spring as they competed at the Jackrabbit Stampede last Friday and […]

title: South Dakota Swimming Awards 2026 SD State Long Course Championship Bid to Rushmore Swim Team and City of Rapid City
description: Rapid City, S.D. — South Dakota Swimming has officially awarded the bid to host the 2026 South Dakota State A Long Course Championships to Rushmore Swim Team (RST) in partnership […]

title: Meade County Commission Chairman Gary Deering Enters Race for District 29 House Seat
description: HEREFORD, S.D. – April 12, 2026 – Gary Deering of Hereford announced today that the South Dakota Secretary of State’s office has received and filed his nominating petitions, officially placing […]

title: Standing Room Only
description: The meaning of life is a question that’s been pondered by philosophers, scientists, artists, and everyone else for most of recorded human history. What is our purpose here? What are […]

title: National and state election law experts to lead free training
description: A free hour-long session includes a discussion about a law requiring proof of citizenship and guidance for election-day news gathering.

description: Seagull Lake in the Boundary Waters. Superior National Forest is home to 20% of all fresh water in the entire national forest system. A congressional vote to allow mining in […]

title: Fink Finishes in Top 10 at NCAA Championship
description: RAPID CITY, SD – South Dakota Mines student-athlete Augustus Fink capped off a standout indoor season by securing a ninth-place finish in the weight throw at the NCAA Division II […]

title: The Daily Slice: Friday, April 17, 2026
description: The Daily Slice, your Black Hills news podcast in under 10 minutes. Delivered every morning Monday-Friday at homesliceaudio.com/dailyslice Man Accused in Monte Gakin Killing Pleads Not Guilty, DNA Testing Delayed […]

title: “A Place For Meow” Of The Week
description: RAPID CITY, S.D. — Pixie has been waiting for a home for months, and her story is one that is hard to forget. When Pixie first came to A Place […]

title: Wanna bet? Washington steps up scrutiny of prediction markets
description: A controversy has erupted over prediction markets such as Polymarket and Kalshi. On April 3, bets were placed on Polymarket on when a U.S. airman would be rescued from Iran. After Rep. Seth Moulton criticized this, Polymarket halted the betting. Well-timed trades have sparked bipartisan concern in Washington about the ethics and regulation of prediction markets. Lawmakers are proposing bills to prevent insider trading and regulate these markets. The Commodity Futures Trading Commission is also under scrutiny for its oversight role. The debate continues on how to address these markets effectively.

title: Yellow Jackets Head to Colorado Springs for Crucial Series Versus Mountain Lions
description: The Black Hills State Yellow Jackets (20-23 (17-15 RMAC)) are continuing their stretch of crucial RMAC series as we are nearing the end of the season. This week, the Green […]

title: Statue honors Boston Marathon pioneer Bobbi Gibb. She’s not just the subject—she’s the sculptor, too
description: There was only one sculptor qualified to capture the combination of pain and satisfaction that Bobbi Gibb felt when she became the first woman to complete the Boston Marathon. It was Gibb herself. The artist, lawyer and athletic pioneer helped unveil her self-portrait in bronze this spring alongside the Hopkinton Town Green, just a few steps from the marathon starting line. The statue is the first on the course of a woman . It will greet the 30,000-plus runners who line up for the race on Patriots’ Day. More than 14,000 of them will be women following Gibb's footsteps.

title: The Lakers still have hope for the playoffs, even without Luka Doncic and Austin Reaves for now
description: The Lakers spent six months building and working toward the opportunity to make some noise in the playoffs. And then Los Angeles’ hopes and plans all collapsed during one quarter in Oklahoma City earlier this month. Luka Doncic and Austin Reaves both left that game with injuries that still haven't healed. That means the Lakers are major underdogs in their first-round series against the Houston Rockets starting this weekend. Not even the ageless presence of LeBron James suggests any way out of this jam for Los Angeles. But Marcus Smart and the Lakers' veterans still believe they've got a shot with toughness and togetherness.

title: House rushes to overnight vote to renew key surveillance tool used by US spy agencies
description: House Republican leadership is rushing early Friday to renew a controversial surveillance program used by U.S. spy agencies. They unveiled substantial changes after days of negotiating with a bloc of Republican holdouts. Leaders have called lawmakers back to session for a middle-of-the-night vote. The substantial changes are far from the clean renewal President Donald Trump had previously demanded. At the center of the standoff is Section 702 of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act, which permits the CIA, National Security Agency, FBI and other agencies to collect and analyze vast amounts of overseas communications without a warrant. In doing so, they can incidentally sweep up communications involving Americans who interact with foreign targets.

title: Georgia Democrats try to make their move in a jumbled, low-dollar primary for governor
description: Democrats running for governor in Georgia are scrambling for attention and cash. The political environment may favor a Democrat to win Georgia's top post for the first time since 1998. But Republicans have spent nearly $100 million in advertising so far, while Democrats have spent only $1.24 million. Most observers believe no Democrat will win a majority in the May 19 primary. Former Atlanta Mayor Keisha Lance Bottoms could advance to a June 16 runoff, thanks in part to superior name identification. The scramble for a second spot is wide open. Contenders include former Republican Lt. Gov. Geoff Duncan, former state Sen. Jason Esteves and former state labor commissioner Mike Thurmond.

title: Trump draws Marie Antoinette comparisons as he leans into the gilded trappings of the presidency
byline: MICHELLE L. PRICE and WILL WEISSERT
description: President Donald Trump is more overtly leaning into some of the spoils of his office in his second term, drawing comparisons to French Queen Marie Antoinette from political opponents. He has celebrated renderings of his $400 million White House ballroom even during the war in Iran and a partial government shutdown. His administration is pushing ahead with plans to build a 250-foot Triumphal Arch near the Lincoln Memorial. Democrats say Trump is more interested in the gilded trappings of the presidency than in everyday Americans' concerns about affordability. The White House says the projects “will benefit generations of future presidents and American visitors.”

title: ICE acting director Todd Lyons will resign at end of May, DHS says
description: U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement acting director Todd Lyons, a key executor of President Donald Trump’s mass deportations agenda, will resign at the end of May, federal officials announced Thursday. Homeland Security Secretary Markwayne Mullin described Lyons as a great leader of ICE. “We wish him luck on his next opportunity in the private sector,” Mullin said in a statement. White House spokesperson Abigail Jackson described Lyons in a post on X as “an American patriot who made our country safer.” The Department of Homeland Security did not immediately respond to an email from The Associated Press asking why he is resigning.

title: US House Dems at ag hearing excoriate Trump cuts proposed for farm and food aid
description: U.S. Agriculture Secretary Brooke Rollins, speaking at a Future Farmers of America event Aug. 18, 2025, at the Tennessee State Fair. (Photo by John Partipilo/Tennessee Lookout) Democrats on a U.S. […]

title: Lawmakers gathered quietly to talk about AI. Angst and fears of ‘destruction’ followed
description: Lawmakers on Capitol Hill conducted a roundtable with leading AI executives and academics to discuss the potential transformative impacts of the technology on American society. The roundtable served as a chance for lawmakers to ask questions of leading AI experts and to express their hopes and concerns about the transformative technology. Several leaders expressed alarm at how the technology is already being used by the Trump administration. Others warned that Congress was doing little to prepare the country for the transformative effects of the technology.

description: Rapid City Central Cobblers Track Preview   Rapid City, SD — Rapid City Central’s head track coach Jacquee Schaefer sat down with the Rapid City Sports Association to give a […]

title: Boyd Posts School Record Time in 3,000m Steeplechase in Day One of California Trip
description: The Black Hills State University men’s and women’s track & field teams were back in action in the first day of a trip to southern California for distance runners, competing […]

title: Singer D4vd is arrested on suspicion of killing of a 14-year-old girl found dead in his Tesla
description: Singer D4vd has been arrested on suspicion of killing of a 14-year-old girl whose decomposing and dismembered body was found in his Tesla. Police said the singer whose legal name is David Burke is being held without bail and they'll present a case to prosecutors on Monday. Burke had been under investigation by an LA County grand jury in secret. He was revealed as a target when his family members objected in a Texas court to being subpoenaed to testify. Investigators say they found the long-dead body of Celeste Rivas Hernandez in the car in a tow yard in September.

title: Yellow Jackets Record a Dozen Event Titles in Bauer Open
description: The Black Hills State University men’s and women’s track & field teams returned to the Black Hills of South Dakota on Wednesday, competing at the annual Bauer Open at South […]

title: Guardians rookie Parker Messick has no-hitter through 6 innings against Orioles
description: Cleveland Guardians rookie Parker Messick has a no-hitter through six innings against the Baltimore Orioles. The 25-year-old has thrown 72 pitches and struck out eight with two walks. The Guardians lead 3-0. Messick walked leadoff hitter Taylor Ward before retiring the next 15 batters. Ward hit a deep flyball to center field in the third inning that Cleveland’s Steven Kwan caught at the wall, and José Ramírez made a nice stop on a grounder by Coby Mayo deep in the hole at third to end the fifth inning. Messick walked Leody Taveras leading off the sixth. The left-hander is attempting to throw the majors’ first no-hitter this season.

title: Democrat Analilia Mejia and Republican Joe Hathaway compete for suburban New Jersey House seat
description: Analilia Mejia will try to expand Democratic momentum in New Jersey as she and Republican Joe Hathaway compete in a special election for the U.S. House. The prize in Thursday's contest is the seat vacated by Mikie Sherrill when she was elected governor last year. Mejia is a former head of the Working Families Alliance who is endorsed by Vermont U.S. Sen. Bernie Sanders. She emerged from a crowded Democratic primary in February. Hathaway is a council member in suburban Randolph. He was unchallenged in the GOP contest. Mejia has been critical of President Donald Trump. Hathaway has seized on Mejia’s progressive credentials, and national Republicans cast her as a socialist.

title: Bronny and LeBron James are looking forward to postseason playing time together with the Lakers
description: LeBron James and Bronny James seem likely to play postseason minutes together this month when the Los Angeles Lakers face the Houston Rockets in the first round. The greatest scorer in NBA history and his 21-year-old son have been teammates for nearly two years, but the prospect of accomplishing a new first is still special to both generations. Bronny’s playing time has increased over the last month, and he has been a rotation piece for the Lakers since Luka Doncic and Austin Reaves went down with injuries two weeks ago. LeBron takes an obvious fatherly pride in Bronny’s development into a contributor for their short-handed team.

title: Trump administration restores funding to Manhattan subway project after NY sues
description: The Trump administration has agreed to resume funding a key Manhattan subway project after New York officials sued. The U.S. Department of Transportation said in a federal court filing Thursday that it has completed its review of the Second Avenue subway project and will begin reimbursing state transit officials for construction costs. The Metropolitan Transportation Authority that's overseeing the project filed suit last month claiming the federal government had breached its contract. The Trump administration withheld almost $60 million in federal funding for building new subway stations in Manhattan over concerns about diversity, equity, and inclusion, or DEI, policies

description: RAPID CITY, SD — South Park Elementary has found its way to national attention following the nomination of first grade teacher Megan Knutson for the prestigious “America’s Favorite Teacher” Award. […]

title: A look at investigations into federal officers months after immigration crackdown in Minnesota
description: Minnesota officials are pushing forward with investigations of federal law enforcement officers involved in the Trump administration's immigration crackdown in the Twin Cities. Immigration and Customs Enforcement agent Gregory Donnell Morgan Jr. is being sought on two counts of aggravated assault. He is accused of pointing his service weapon at people in a car on a Minneapolis highway. Hennepin County Attorney Mary Moriarty is investigating whether he acted outside his authority as a federal officer and a warrant is out for his arrest. Minnesota has also sued the Trump administration to provide evidence in cases involving shootings by federal officers.

title: Ludvig Aberg cleans up his game and leads at Hilton Head with a 63
description: Ludvig Aberg has the early lead at the RBC Heritage with a bogey-free 63 at Harbour Town. The Swede is coming off a disappointing week at the Masters by his standards. He tied for 21st after two previous years in the top 10. Aberg says he wasn't worried because his swing felt good. He just made some silly mistakes. That wasn't the case on a warm day along the Calibogue Sound. He was one shot ahead of Harris English and Viktor Hovland. Scottie Scheffler played in the afternoon and appeared to have settled down after a wild start.

title: Suspect enters new guilty plea in the case of missing Navajo grandmother Ella Mae Begay
description: A man charged in the disappearance of a Navajo grandmother has pleaded guilty to robbery as part of a second plea agreement. Preston Henry Tolth could face a maximum of five years in federal prison, with credit for three years already served, if a judge signs off. Ella Mae Begay vanished in 2021 from Sweetwater, Arizona. Her case has highlighted the disproportionate violence faced by Native Americans. Tolth admitted to using force to take her truck and leaving her on the side of the road. Begay’s family opposes the agreement, saying prosecutors ignored their wishes. A judge is set to review the agreement in May. The U.S. Attorney’s Office declined to comment.

title: US urges nations to back ‘trade over aid’ plan as UN warns against privatizing assistance
description: The U.S. is urging other nations to back a “trade over aid” initiative at the United Nations as part of the Trump administration’s broader shift away from donor-focused development assistance and toward greater private investment. The U.N. is warning against privatizing a global aid system that delivers crucial assistance to some of the world’s most vulnerable populations. Ahead of the initiative being formally introduced at the U.N. this month, Secretary of State Marco Rubio has ordered all U.S. diplomats to deliver a call to action to high-level foreign officials to sign on with their support by Monday. That's according to a diplomatic cable sent this week and obtained by The Associated Press.

title: With GOP defections, US House passes bill extending legal status for 350,000 Haitians
description: Massachusetts Democratic U.S. Rep. Ayanna Pressley speaks at a press conference April 15, 2026, outside the U.S. Capitol in Washington, D.C. From left to right just in back of her […]

title: South Dakotan tells Congress of ‘growing water security risk’ if pipeline project is delayed
description: Kristin Conzet, R-Rapid City, is pictured in the South Dakota House of Representatives on March 5, 2024, during her time as a legislator. She is now the executive director of […]

title: In address on media ethics, former Washington Post editor worries about fading moral compass
description: During a contentious era, one of journalism's leading figures says he's concerned about news outlets that may be losing their moral compass. Former Washington Post editor Marty Baron, during a speech about media ethics this week, singled out breaches by leaders of CBS News, advocacy journalists and also mainstream reporters who failed to aggressively cover former President Joe Biden's fitness for office in the early part of this decade. It's a pressure-filled environment: less than 24 hours after Baron talked at a New York University event, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth was criticizing reporters for allegedly rooting against the United States with its questioning of the Iran war.
